mnemonics: CPU Can Process Instructions Very Rapidly (CPU, Memory, I/O, Bus, Control Unit, ALU, Registers, Program Counter)
keyPoints: The von Neumann architecture is the fundamental design upon which most modern computers are based.
The central processing unit (CPU) is the brain of the computer and consists of a control unit (CU) and an arithmetic logic unit (ALU).
The system bus is the communication highway that connects the major components (CPU, Memory, I/O) and consists of the address bus, data bus, and control bus.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the address bus (carries memory location) and the data bus (carries the actual data).
Forgetting that the control unit (CU) does not perform calculations; it fetches, decodes, and manages the execution of instructions, while the ALU handles the arithmetic and logic operations.
